$comment: Permission boundary for the unattended overnight loop ONLY. Loaded via `claude -p --settings`; your interactive settings (~/.claude/settings.json, .claude/settings.local.json) are untouched. DENY beats ALLOW — the deny list is the real safety boundary. make targets are whitelisted explicitly (no blanket `Bash(make *)`) so a newly-added destructive target is NOT auto-allowed. ADAPT PER REPO: add your project's gate targets to allow, and your destructive/online targets (db/infra/deploy/serve) to deny.
